summary: Kenneth Suna demonstrates how to generate significant weekly premium income by selling covered calls on high-priced stocks, using real examples ranging from $256/week (Nvidia) to $9,500/month (AutoZone). The strategy requires owning 100 shares per contract and targets wealthy investors building generational wealth through consistent weekly or monthly premium collection.
takeaways: Covered calls require 100 shares minimum per contract
Weekly expirations allow premium collection every single week
High-priced stocks generate substantially larger premiums: Nvidia $256, Microsoft $430, Tesla $875, Broadcom $1,400, Booking Holdings $4,900/week
AutoZone generates ~$9,500 monthly but only offers monthly contracts (not weekly)
Strike prices are typically set near current stock price (e.g., Nvidia at $187.50 strike for $18,500 stock cost)

strategy_candidates.change: Identify and prioritize weekly-expiration high-priced stocks ($44k-$50k+ per 100 shares) to maximize Brian's $50k portfolio rotation efficiency and weekly premium targets toward $1,250/mo goal
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth emphasizes weekly expirations allow 'making this money every single week' and shows Broadcom/Tesla/Booking Holdings generating $1,400-$4,900 weekly, directly scalable to Brian's $50k capital and premium targets

title: How to make BIG money selling covered calls #shorts
